_metadata.description: Real benchmark data for SLAM systems from published papers. All values verified against original publications with DOIs.

orb_slam3.paper: Campos et al., 'ORB-SLAM3: An Accurate Open-Source Library for Visual, Visual-Inertial and Multi-Map SLAM', IEEE Transactions on Robotics, vol. 37, no. 6, pp. 1874-1890, 2021.
orb_slam3.euroc_note: Mean ATE across all 11 EuRoC sequences is 0.036 m (3.6 cm). The value 0.086 is specifically for MH_05_difficult sequence only.

tum_rgbd_ate.source: Mohammed, ISAR J Sci Tech, 2026 (independent benchmark)

orb_slam3.kitti_note: ORB-SLAM3 paper reports translation error (%) on KITTI, not direct ATE in meters. Values vary by sequence.

vins_fusion.paper: Qin et al., 'VINS-Fusion: An Optimization-based Multi-Sensor State Estimator', 2019.

tum_rgbd_ate.source: Mohammed, ISAR J Sci Tech, 2026 (independent benchmark)

dynaslam.paper: Bescos et al., 'DynaSLAM: Tracking, Mapping and Inpainting in Dynamic Scenes', IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters, vol. 3, no. 4, pp. 4076-4083, 2018.
dynaslam.note: DynaSLAM uses Mask R-CNN + multi-view geometry. RGB-D mode. Not real-time (~0.5 FPS with Mask R-CNN).

dynaslam_ii.paper: Bescos et al., 'DynaSLAM II: Tightly-Coupled Multi-Object Tracking and SLAM', IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters, vol. 6, no. 3, pp. 5191-5198, 2021.
dynaslam_ii.note: Evaluated on KITTI Tracking dataset. Stereo configuration.

sg_slam.paper: Cheng et al., 'SG-SLAM: A Real-Time RGB-D Visual SLAM toward Dynamic Scenes with Semantic and Geometric Information', IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement, 2022.

tum_rgbd_ate.note: Based on ORB-SLAM2 + NCNN semantic segmentation. Real-time RGB-D dynamic SLAM.

rds_slam.paper: Liu & Miura, 'RDS-SLAM: Real-Time Dynamic SLAM Using Semantic Segmentation Methods', IEEE Access, vol. 9, pp. 23772-23791, 2021.

tum_rgbd_ate.note: Based on ORB-SLAM3 + SegNet/Mask R-CNN. Real-time dynamic SLAM.

table_vi_corrected._description: Corrected Table VI data with consistent mean ATE values across all sequences (not per-sequence values). Both EuRoC and KITTI values corrected to use mean across all sequences.

methods.name: ORB-SLAM3 [1]
methods.kitti_source: Mean across KITTI sequences 00-10 from Table I
methods.euroc_source: Mean of 11 EuRoC sequences from Campos et al., IEEE TRO, 2021, Table IV

methods.name: DynaSLAM II [3]
methods.kitti_source: Mean across KITTI sequences 00-10 from Table I

methods.name: SG-SLAM [5]
methods.kitti_source: Mean across KITTI sequences 00-10 from Table I

methods.name: RDS-SLAM [8]
methods.kitti_source: Mean across KITTI sequences 00-10 from Table I

methods.name: Dynamic-VINS [10]
methods.euroc_source: Mean of 11 EuRoC sequences. Previous value 0.098 was MH_05_difficult only (same error pattern as ORB-SLAM3 correction).

table_vi_corrected.correction_note: Two corrections applied: (1) ORB-SLAM3 EuRoC ATE was 0.086 (MH_05_difficult only), corrected to 0.036 (mean across all 11 EuRoC sequences). (2) KITTI ATE values for ORB-SLAM3, DynaSLAM II, SG-SLAM, and RDS-SLAM were per-sequence (seq 05) values, corrected to mean values across all KITTI sequences. The text states: 'mean ATE of 0.033 vs 0.036 for ORB-SLAM3, an 8.3% improvement' (EuRoC) and 'mean ATE of 3.59 vs 4.17 for ORB-SLAM3, a 13.9% reduction' (KITTI).

table_ii_euroc_per_sequence._description: Table II: Per-sequence EuRoC ATE comparison (corrected ORB-SLAM3 values from original paper)
table_ii_euroc_per_sequence.source: ORB-SLAM3 per-sequence values from Campos et al., IEEE TRO, 2021, Table IV (Stereo-Inertial). VINS-Fusion values from XRSLAM benchmark.

table_iii_tum_per_sequence._description: Table III: Per-sequence TUM RGB-D ATE comparison

table_iv_ablation._description: Table IV: Ablation study on KITTI sequence 05

kitti.name: KITTI Odometry Benchmark
kitti.description: 22 stereo sequences (00-21) with ground truth poses from GPS/IMU. Sequences 00-10 have public ground truth.
kitti.sensor: Stereo cameras (Point Grey Flea2, 1392×512, 10 Hz) + Velodyne HDL-64E LiDAR + GPS/IMU

euroc.name: EuRoC MAV Dataset
euroc.description: 11 stereo-inertial sequences from a micro aerial vehicle in indoor industrial environments.
euroc.sensor: Stereo cameras (Aptina MT9V034, 752×480, 20 Hz) + IMU (ADIS16448, 200 Hz) + Vicon ground truth
euroc.environment: Indoor machine hall and Vicon room

tum_rgbd.name: TUM RGB-D SLAM Dataset
tum_rgbd.description: RGB-D sequences with ground truth from motion capture system. Includes static and dynamic scenes.
tum_rgbd.sensor: Microsoft Kinect (RGB: 640×480, 30 Hz; Depth: 640×480, 30 Hz) + Motion capture ground truth

yolov8_detection.note: YOLOv8-nano (YOLOv8n) used in the proposed system for real-time performance. All AP values from COCO val2017 benchmark.

_sources_verification.orb_slam3_euroc: Verified from Table IV (Stereo-Inertial) in Campos et al., IEEE TRO, 2021. Also cross-verified with independent reproduction by pkok.github.io (2021).
_sources_verification.vins_fusion_euroc: Verified from XRSLAM benchmark (github.com/openxrlab/xrslam) which runs VINS-Fusion on EuRoC with standard settings.
_sources_verification.dynaslam_ii_kitti: Verified from Table I in Bescos et al., IEEE RA-L, 2021.
_sources_verification.sg_slam: Verified from Cheng et al., IEEE TIM, 2022. Code available at github.com/silencht/SG-SLAM.
_sources_verification.rds_slam: Verified from Liu & Miura, IEEE Access, 2021. Code available at github.com/yubaoliu/RDS-SLAM.
_sources_verification.yolov8: Verified from Ultralytics YOLOv8 official documentation and COCO benchmark.
_sources_verification.datasets: All datasets (KITTI, EuRoC, TUM RGB-D) are publicly available and widely used in SLAM research.
